title: Indian Food, Festivals and Culture
description: A broader Indian culture round with regional cuisine, seasonal festivals, classical arts, textiles, and traditions.

questions.prompt: Which festival in Kerala is marked by the floral design called pookalam?
questions.hint: It celebrates the annual homecoming of King Mahabali.

questions.prompt: Which Punjabi folk dance is traditionally performed by men during harvest celebrations?
questions.hint: Its female counterpart is gidda.

questions.prompt: Which sweet from Odisha and Bengal sparked a long-running origin debate and is made from chhena?
questions.hint: It is soaked in sugar syrup.

questions.prompt: What is the principal grain used in pongal, the South Indian festive dish from which the festival takes one of its names?
questions.hint: It is cooked with lentils in the savory version.

questions.prompt: Which classical dance form of Kerala is known for elaborate face makeup and all-night storytelling?
questions.hint: It features green, red, and black facial color coding.

questions.prompt: Which festival in Assam centers on the annual Ambubachi Mela at the Kamakhya Temple?
questions.hint: It is linked with the monsoon and the feminine principle.

questions.prompt: Which Maharashtrian festive flatbread is stuffed with jaggery and lentils?
questions.hint: It is especially popular during Holi and Gudi Padwa season.

questions.prompt: Which textile tradition from Gujarat and Rajasthan uses tie-and-dye techniques and is also called bandhej?
questions.hint: Its name starts with B.

questions.prompt: Which classical dance form is associated with the temples of Odisha?
questions.hint: Its tribhangi stance is distinctive.

questions.prompt: Which harvest festival of Tamil Nadu includes the day Mattu Pongal for honoring cattle?
questions.hint: It is celebrated in January.

questions.prompt: Which Kashmiri multi-course feast is known as wazwan?
questions.hint: It is especially associated with weddings and formal banquets.

questions.prompt: Which instrument is traditionally associated with the Bismillah Khan and often heard at temple and wedding ceremonies?
questions.hint: It is a reed instrument.

questions.prompt: Which Rajasthani dance form is performed balancing multiple pots on the head?
questions.hint: It is often seen in desert cultural shows.

questions.prompt: Which festival marks the beginning of the Vikram Samvat new year in parts of western India and is symbolized by a raised banner?
questions.hint: It is especially associated with Maharashtra.

questions.prompt: Which dish from Hyderabad combines rice with meat and is famous for its dum cooking technique?
questions.hint: It is perhaps the city's best-known culinary export.

questions.prompt: Which classical dance form from Andhra Pradesh takes its name from a village in Krishna district?
questions.hint: It was revived in the 20th century by Vempati Chinna Satyam and others.

questions.prompt: Which festival in north India celebrates the bond between brothers and sisters with a sacred thread tied on the wrist?
questions.hint: It falls in the month of Shravana.

questions.prompt: Which Gujarati snack made from gram flour is often steamed and then tempered with mustard seeds?
questions.hint: It is yellow and sponge-like.

questions.prompt: Which dance-drama tradition of Karnataka centers on mythological stories and flamboyant costume?
questions.hint: Its name begins with Y.

questions.prompt: Which autumn festival in West Bengal culminates with the immersion of Goddess Durga's image?
questions.hint: Its main days are Saptami, Ashtami, Navami, and Dashami.

questions.prompt: Which Bengali sweet, shaped like a round fried ball, is associated with Nadia district and often confused with rasgulla?
questions.hint: Its syrup is darker and caramelized.

questions.prompt: Which Indian classical music gharana is strongly associated with Ustad Allauddin Khan and later Ravi Shankar and Ali Akbar Khan?
questions.hint: Its name comes from a princely state in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.prompt: Which festival of lights in Punjab commemorates the release of Guru Hargobind and coincides with Diwali?
questions.hint: Its name begins with Bandi.

questions.prompt: Which rice-based fermented dish from Kerala is served with vegetable stew and is often bowl-shaped?
questions.hint: It is soft in the centre and lacy at the edges.

questions.prompt: Which Indian art tradition from Bihar uses natural dyes and was historically painted on mud walls?
questions.hint: It is also named after a region called Mithila.

questions.prompt: Which spring festival in Manipur features the Thabal Chongba dance and often overlaps with Yaoshang celebrations?
questions.hint: It is sometimes compared to Holi in spirit.

questions.prompt: Which Mughlai dessert from Hyderabad is made from bread, milk, and dry fruits?
questions.hint: Its name literally means sweet dish of bread.

questions.prompt: Which classical dance form of Tamil Nadu grew out of the old Sadir tradition?
questions.hint: Rukmini Devi Arundale played a major role in its revival.

questions.prompt: Which annual fair in Rajasthan is famous for both camel trading and a sacred lake pilgrimage?
questions.hint: It is named after the town where it is held.

questions.prompt: Which Maharashtrian savory snack made from spiced mashed potatoes in a batter is paired with pav?
questions.hint: It is a famous Mumbai street food.

questions.prompt: Which folk theatre form from Uttar Pradesh and adjoining regions combines song, dance, and dramatic narration?
questions.hint: It is a traveling performance tradition with a rhyming name.

questions.prompt: Which Sikh festival commemorates the founding of the Khalsa in 1699?
questions.hint: It is celebrated in April.

questions.prompt: Which Rajasthani dish made from gram flour dumplings in yogurt gravy is often served with bajra roti?
questions.hint: Its name has two words.

questions.prompt: Which weaving tradition of Banaras is especially famous for rich silk sarees with zari work?
questions.hint: It shares its name with the city's older English spelling.

questions.prompt: Which instrument associated with Shivkumar Sharma was adapted to Indian classical music from Kashmiri folk tradition?
questions.hint: It is struck with small mallets.

questions.prompt: Which festival in Goa involves floats, costumes, and revelry just before Lent?
questions.hint: It reflects the state's Portuguese heritage.

questions.prompt: Which Bengali rice-and-fish meal is considered so emblematic that fish features heavily in wedding and ritual customs?
questions.hint: The fish in question is often hilsa or rohu, but the cultural identity is broader.

questions.prompt: Which painting style from Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh is known for intricate devotional depictions of Krishna and nature?
questions.hint: Its name begins with P and is also a princely state school.

questions.prompt: Which Jain festival centers on forgiveness and ends with Kshamavani for many practitioners?
questions.hint: Shvetambara and Digambara traditions observe it differently.

questions.prompt: Which Mangalorean dish pairs crisp dried rice wafers with spicy chicken curry?
questions.hint: Its name has two words, the second meaning bread or roti.

questions.prompt: Which devotional song tradition of Maharashtra and north Karnataka is associated with Vithoba worship and the Varkari movement?
questions.hint: Many are composed by Tukaram and Namdev.

questions.prompt: Which New Year festival in Kashmir is traditionally called Navreh?
questions.hint: It is observed by Kashmiri Pandits.

questions.prompt: Which millet flatbread from Maharashtra is often eaten with thecha or pithla?
questions.hint: It is thicker than a chapati and made by hand patting.

questions.prompt: Which martial-art performance tradition from Kerala combines combat training with ritual movement?
questions.hint: It is often cited among the world's oldest fighting systems.

questions.prompt: Which festival in Bihar and eastern Uttar Pradesh involves offering arghya to the setting and rising sun?
questions.hint: It is observed with strict fasting and riverbank rituals.

questions.prompt: Which sweet dish from Karnataka made with lentils, jaggery, coconut, and cardamom is a festive filling for obbattu?
questions.hint: It shares a family resemblance with puran in puran poli.
questions.answer: Holige or obbattu filling of bele hoorna is accepted; main answer: Holige

questions.prompt: Which north Indian string instrument is most associated with Ustad Vilayat Khan and Pt. Ravi Shankar's repertoire tradition?
questions.hint: It has long frets and sympathetic strings.

questions.prompt: Which annual dance festival in Odisha is held against the illuminated backdrop of the Konark Sun Temple?
questions.hint: It shares its name with the monument town.

questions.prompt: Which festival in Tamil Nadu marks the raising of a ceremonial kalasam and elaborate kolam patterns during the Margazhi season dedicated to Andal?
questions.hint: It culminates around the month of January and is associated with devotional hymns.
questions.answer: Margazhi festival or Andal-related Margazhi observances

questions.prompt: Which Lucknowi rice dish is lighter than biryani and cooked with stock, aromatic spices, and meat?
questions.hint: Its name begins with Y and is associated with Awadhi cuisine.
